title: GA/P-011: authorship-first done-guard in blackboard.py (kills the false-refusal override friction)
description: Owned file: .harness/bin/blackboard.py ONLY. Restructure the done-guard in cmd_update (~:288-296): AUTHORSHIP governs, not current status. New semantics: '--status done' is ALLOWED whenever a handoff record exists AND args.agent != handoff.from (regardless of current status - a verifier who claimed the task, or set in_progress, may verdict). REFUSED when: no handoff record exists (task never went through review - message must say so), OR args.agent == handoff.from (producer of record - message must say so). --override-producer-check (+mandatory --note) remains the escape for exactly those two refusal cases; producer_check_refused/overridden events keep the explicit agent field; 'failed' stays ungated; refusal messages name the ACTUAL reason. Acceptance (fresh scratch task T-099, epic scratch, dispose failed after): (1) claim X + in_progress + self-done -> REFUSED (no handoff); (2) handoff by X, done by X -> REFUSED (producer); (3) handoff by X, CLAIM by Y, done by Y -> ACCEPTED, NO override needed [the P-011 measurable - exits 0]; (4) handoff by X, Y claims + in_progress, done by Y -> ACCEPTED; (5) override still works with --note, refused without; (6) regression: normal cycle, py_compile, status/show/next unchanged; (7) historical board untouched.

notes.note: plan: restructure cmd_update's done-guard (bb.py:279-315) to authorship-first — refuse only when no handoff record exists, or when args.agent == handoff.from; current status (claimed/in_progress/review) no longer gates. Preserve override-producer-check escape + event logging. Validate with fresh scratch task T-099 across 7 probes, py_compile, and live regression via my own claim/in_progress/handoff/done calls on T-036 itself.

DECISION: restructured cmd_update's done-guard (was :288 status-check firing before :296 authorship-check) into authorship-first order — refusal now fires only on (a) handoff is None (no review ever happened) or (b) args.agent == handoff.from (producer of record), full stop; previous_status is no longer part of the gate condition (still reported in the refusal message text for context). This directly fixes the false-refusal class from ev604/605, ev617/618: a verifier who claims a review task (status -> claimed) or sets in_progress on it can now verdict done with exit 0, no override. DECISION: reset-to-open (update --status open) still nulls handoff (pre-existing behavior, unchanged) so a reopened task always requires a fresh handoff before it can be closed — authorship-first does not weaken that. DEVIATION: none from the task contract; all 7 acceptance probes run verbatim against fresh scratch T-099 (epic=scratch), disposed --status failed with closure note after use. Regression: py_compile clean before and after; status/show/next output format unchanged; failed-path remains ungated; a full normal claim->in_progress->handoff->claim->in_progress->done cycle succeeds with exit 0. Historical board (T-000..T-035, T-090..T-098) untouched -- only T-036 (my own in_progress) and sibling-owned T-037/T-038 (concurrent workers, disjoint files) show non-done status, T-099 disposed failed. events.jsonl confirms producer_check_refused/overridden fire with explicit agent field for probes 1, 2, 5a/5b.

HANDOFF -> verifier: P-011 authorship-first done-guard implemented in .harness/bin/blackboard.py cmd_update (~lines 279-315 relocated to ~281-313). Restructured: refusal now fires ONLY when (a) t['handoff'] is None (no review ever happened) or (b) args.agent == handoff['from'] (producer of record) -- previous_status no longer gates. Replay (all against fresh scratch task, dispose --status failed after; use distinct agent ids to avoid clashing with mine):
  python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py add-task --id T-0XX --title probe --role worker --engine any --priority 9 --epic scratch --agent verifier-id
  P1 self-done-no-handoff -> REFUSED: claim T-0XX(x); update T-0XX --status in_progress(x); update T-0XX --status done(x) => exit 1 'no handoff record exists'. OBSERVED (my run, agent probe-agent-x): exit 1, message exactly that.
  P2 handoff+self-done -> REFUSED: handoff T-0XX --to-role verifier(x); update T-0XX --status done(x) => exit 1 'was handed off by ... the producer cannot verdict'. OBSERVED: exit 1 as expected.
  P3 [P-011 measurable] handoff by X, CLAIM by Y (status becomes 'claimed', NOT review), done by Y => exit 0, NO override needed. OBSERVED: claim T-0XX(y) then update T-0XX --status done(y) -> exit 0, no WARNING line, task done. THIS is the fix: old code would have refused here since previous_status='claimed'!='review'.
  P4 handoff by X, Y claims + in_progress, done by Y => exit 0. OBSERVED: exit 0.
  P5 override without --note => exit 1 'requires --note'; with --note => exit 0 + WARNING + producer_check_overridden event. OBSERVED both.
  P6 regression: python3 -m py_compile .harness/bin/blackboard.py => 0 output (clean); status/show/next output format unchanged (spot-checked); failed-path stays ungated (claim -> straight to failed, no handoff, exit 0); one full normal open->claim->in_progress->handoff->claim(other)->in_progress(other)->done(other) cycle => exit 0 throughout.
  P7 historical board untouched:  before/after shows T-000..T-035 all still 'done' with unchanged fields; only T-036 (mine, in_progress) and sibling-owned T-037/T-038 (concurrent, unrelated) plus disposed T-099 changed.
  grep -E 'producer_check_(refused|overridden)' .harness/logs/events.jsonl | tail -5  -- confirms explicit agent field on every refusal/override, including probes 1/2/5.
Scratch T-099 used for the above, disposed --status failed with closure note (see .harness/tasks/T-099.json). Diff is additive/restructuring only inside cmd_update's done-status branch plus 3 comment/docstring blocks describing the new semantics; no other command touched. NOTE FOR YOU (the verifier): this task's own handoff record has from=substrate-worker-1 -- you should be able to claim T-036, optionally set in_progress, and then run `update T-036 --status done` directly with NO --override-producer-check needed, since you are a different agent than the producer of record. If that requires an override for you, the fix has NOT met its own acceptance bar -- flag it.

CORRECTION (shell backtick ate a command in the prior handoff note's P7 line): the omitted command was: python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py status -- run before/after the probe sequence to diff historical task rows.

VERIFIED (verifier-b, gen-3 P-011 re-attack): replayed 7 producer probes (P1 no-handoff REFUSED, P2 self-done REFUSED, P3 measurable handoff-X/claim-Y/done-Y EXIT0 no override, P4 EXIT0, P5a override-no-note REFUSED, P5b override+note EXIT0+event, P6 py_compile clean, P7 historical board IDENTICAL). Re-ran full T-031 attack table: done-from open/claimed/blocked/in_progress with no handoff all REFUSED; self-done-after-handoff REFUSED; ambient main==main slip REFUSED; override gate intact. New surface: (a) reopen+reclaim+rehandoff by original producer -> REFUSED producer (handoff.from reset to X); (d) rehandoff by Y makes Y producer-of-record, X passes (matches T-031 INFO note); (b) re-done of done task and (c) failed->done both EXIT0 by non-producer -- LOW-severity coherence consequences of dropping the status gate, NOT producer!=approver bypasses (still require existing handoff + non-producer actor); (e) empty-string handoff.from via --agent '' is internally consistent (actor==''->refused, actor!=''->allowed), fail-open only under self-inflicted empty producer id, unreachable via default 'main'. events carry explicit agent on every refusal/override incl empty. Docstring+refusal messages match new authorship-first behavior. Split-note backtick correction benign. This verdict itself is the P-011 proof: done-from-claimed by non-producer, exit 0, no override.
